name: Pennsylvania SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Provides monthly food assistance benefits to eligible low-income individuals and families via an EBT card to purchase groceries.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 160% of the Federal Poverty Level for most households
hours: County Assistance Offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: COMPASS - Pennsylvania's Benefits Portal
description: Online portal to apply for and manage health and human service programs including SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, LIHEAP, CHIP, and Child Care Works.
eligibilitySummary: Any Pennsylvania resident seeking to apply for or manage public benefits
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Online portal available 24/7

name: Pennsylvania Medicaid (Medical Assistance)
description: Provides free or low-cost health coverage to eligible low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly individuals, and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly individuals, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults: up to 138% FPL; Children: up to 319% FPL; Pregnant women: up to 220% FPL
hours: Consumer Service Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennie - Pennsylvania Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Pennsylvania's official state-based health insurance marketplace where individuals and families can shop for and enroll in quality health and dental plans, often with financial assistance to lower costs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families without employer-sponsored or government health coverage
incomeLimitNotes: Financial assistance available for households with income between 100-400% FPL
hours: Customer Service: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides free or low-cost health insurance coverage for uninsured children and teens under age 19 who are not eligible for Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children and teens under 19 not eligible for Medical Assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Free CHIP for families with income up to about 218% FPL; subsidized CHIP available at higher incomes
hours: CHIP Helpline: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania Unemployment Compensation (UC)
description: Provides temporary income support to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own or had their hours significantly reduced.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient qualifying wages in their base year
incomeLimitNotes: Must have minimum of 18 credit weeks and sufficient qualifying wages in base year
hours: UC Service Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

name: PA CareerLink - Workforce Development
description: Statewide network of career centers providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, skills training, and employer connections for job seekers.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Pennsylvania job seekers and employers
hours: Varies by location; most offices Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA)
description: Provides affordable homeownership and rental housing options for low- and moderate-income Pennsylvanians through mortgage programs, rental assistance, and housing counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Low- and moderate-income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county
hours: Customer Solutions Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania Homeowner Assistance Fund (PAHAF)
description: Provides financial assistance to eligible homeowners who have experienced pandemic-related financial hardship to help with mortgage payments, property taxes, and other housing costs.
eligibilitySummary: Homeowners who experienced financial hardship after January 21, 2020 due to the pandemic
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of the area median income
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides cash grants to help low-income households pay their heating bills. Grants range from $200 to $1,000 and are paid directly to the utility provider.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households that meet federal income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level
hours: Application period: November through April annually

name: Pennsylvania Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
description: Increases energy efficiency in homes by providing free insulation, air sealing, heating system repairs or replacement, and other energy-saving improvements to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters with landlord approval
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level
hours: Contact local weatherization agency for hours

name: Pennsylvania Legal Aid Network (PLAN)
description: Statewide consortium of independent legal aid programs coordinating the delivery of free civil legal assistance to low-income Pennsylvanians in areas like housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals who cannot afford legal representation in civil matters
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125-200% FPL depending on the program
hours: Varies by local legal aid office

name: PALawHELP.org
description: Free online legal information resource helping Pennsylvanians understand their legal rights, find legal forms, and locate legal aid programs in their area.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Pennsylvanians seeking legal information and resources
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: Disability Rights Pennsylvania
description: Federally mandated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal help and advocacy for people with disabilities on issues including employment, education, housing, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Pennsylvania experiencing disability-related legal issues
hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: PA 211
description: Free, confidential, 24/7 service connecting Pennsylvanians with local health and human services including utility assistance, housing, food, childcare, and crisis support across all 67 counties.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Pennsylvania residents seeking community resources and services
languages: English, Spanish, and 75+ languages via chat translation

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline - Pennsylvania
description: 24/7 crisis support for anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crises, or emotional distress. Available by phone call, text, or online chat.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in crisis or emotional distress, available to all regardless of income

name: TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) - Pennsylvania
description: Provides temporary cash assistance to eligible low-income families with children to help cover basic needs while working toward self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children who are US citizens and Pennsylvania residents
incomeLimitNotes: Resources must have a value of $1,000 or less; income limits vary by family size
hours: County Assistance Offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Pennsylvania General Assistance (GA)
description: State-funded cash assistance program for low-income individuals who do not qualify for TANF, including those with temporary or permanent disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals with disabilities, caretakers of disabled persons, or those meeting other specific criteria who do not qualify for TANF
incomeLimitNotes: Resources of $1,000 or less; benefits up to $205/month for individuals
hours: County Assistance Offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Child Care Works (CCW) - Pennsylvania
description: Subsidized child care program that helps low-income families pay for quality child care, enabling parents to work or attend education and training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families who are working, in education, or in job training programs
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% FPL at initial eligibility; up to 235% FPL for continued eligibility
hours: Contact local Early Learning Resource Center (ELRC) for hours

name: Pennsylvania WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Provides supplemental nutrition, health screenings, and nutrition education for pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants; and children up to age 5 who meet income and nutrition risk criteria
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level
hours: WIC office hours vary by county location

name: Philabundance
description: Largest hunger relief organization in the Delaware Valley, distributing food to a network of partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and soup kitchens across the Philadelphia region.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing food insecurity in the Philadelphia region
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Greater Pittsburgh Community Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank distributing millions of pounds of food annually to partner agencies across southwestern Pennsylvania to fight hunger.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in southwestern Pennsylvania
hours: Mon, Wed, Fri 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Tue, Thu 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Sat 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM

name: Central Pennsylvania Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank serving 27 counties in central Pennsylvania, distributing food through a network of partner agencies and Healthy Food Hubs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing food insecurity in central Pennsylvania's 27-county service area
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Lehigh Valley and Northeast Pennsylvania
description: Feeding America member food bank serving six counties in the Lehigh Valley and northeast Pennsylvania region, providing food to partner agencies and direct distribution programs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in Lehigh, Northampton, Carbon, Monroe, Pike, and Wayne counties
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

name: Feeding Pennsylvania
description: Statewide association of Feeding America-affiliated food banks coordinating hunger relief efforts, securing funding, and advocating for food-insecure Pennsylvanians.
eligibilitySummary: Connects individuals to local food banks and pantries across Pennsylvania
hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: SEPTA Reduced Fare Program
description: Provides half-fare discounts on all SEPTA transit services for riders with disabilities, plus two free transfers within 120 minutes.
eligibilitySummary: Riders with qualifying disabilities in the SEPTA service area (Bucks, Chester, Delaware, Montgomery, and Philadelphia counties)
hours: Walk-ins: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:45 PM; appointments recommended

name: SEPTA Senior Fare Program
description: Provides free transit on all SEPTA bus, trolley, and Metro routes for seniors age 65 and older, plus free Regional Rail rides within Pennsylvania.
eligibilitySummary: Pennsylvania residents age 65 and older in the SEPTA service area
hours: Walk-ins: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:45 PM

name: Philadelphia Zero Fare Transit Program
description: City of Philadelphia pilot program providing free SEPTA transit passes to low-income residents living near or below the poverty level.
eligibilitySummary: Philadelphia residents with income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level; participants selected via lottery
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 150% FPL

name: Pennsylvania Department of Military and Veterans Affairs (DMVA)
description: Provides state-level benefits and services for Pennsylvania's veterans and their families, including financial assistance, employment help, long-term care, and connections to federal VA benefits.
address: Bldg. 0-47, Fort Indiantown Gap, Annville, PA 17003
eligibilitySummary: Pennsylvania veterans and their eligible family members
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

name: PA VETConnect
description: Pennsylvania initiative connecting veterans and their families to thousands of resources including benefits, employment, education, housing, and health services.
eligibilitySummary: Pennsylvania veterans and their family members seeking connections to available resources
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

name: BenePhilly - Philadelphia Benefits Enrollment
description: Free one-on-one assistance helping Philadelphia residents screen for and enroll in local, state, and federal public benefit programs including SNAP, Medicaid, LIHEAP, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Philadelphia residents who may qualify for public benefits programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by individual benefit program
hours: Helpline hours vary; centers have walk-in and appointment options

name: Philadelphia Department of Human Services
description: City child welfare and juvenile justice agency providing child protective services, foster care, adoption services, and family support programs for Philadelphia families.
eligibilitySummary: Philadelphia families and children in need of child welfare, protective services, and family support
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; 24-hour hotline for child abuse reports

name: Philadelphia Office of Homeless Services
description: City agency providing homelessness prevention, emergency shelter, transitional housing, and supportive services for people experiencing or at risk of homelessness.
eligibilitySummary: Philadelphia residents who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless
hours: Homelessness Prevention InfoLine: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Community Legal Services of Philadelphia
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Philadelphians in areas including housing, public benefits, employment, family law, consumer protection, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Philadelphia residents needing civil legal help
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% FPL
hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Philadelphia Legal Assistance (PLA)
description: Provides free legal help to low-income Philadelphia residents with unemployment compensation, tax issues, family law, housing, and other civil legal matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Philadelphia residents who cannot afford a private attorney
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% FPL
hours: General Intake: Mon-Thu 9:30 AM - 12:00 PM; Save Your Home Philly Hotline: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: PA CareerLink Philadelphia
description: Philadelphia-based workforce development center offering job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, resume workshops, and employer connections for city residents.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Philadelphia job seekers and employers
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania Property Tax/Rent Rebate Program
description: Provides rebates of $380 to $1,000 on property taxes or rent paid by eligible older adults and people with disabilities, funded by the Pennsylvania Lottery.
eligibilitySummary: Pennsylvanians age 65+, widows/widowers age 50+, or people with disabilities age 18+ who are homeowners or renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income of $48,110 or less annually (excludes half of Social Security benefits)
hours: Applications accepted January through June 30 annually

name: PACE/PACENET Prescription Assistance Program
description: State-funded prescription drug assistance programs for seniors age 65+, helping lower out-of-pocket medication costs in coordination with Medicare Part D plans.
eligibilitySummary: Pennsylvania residents age 65 and older meeting income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: PACE: single income up to $14,500, married up to $17,700; PACENET: single income $14,500-$27,500, married $17,700-$35,500
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania Department of Aging
description: State agency overseeing programs and services for older Pennsylvanians through a network of 52 Area Agencies on Aging, including meals, caregiver support, health and wellness, elder abuse reporting, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Older Pennsylvanians (generally age 60+) and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; many services available regardless of income
hours: Elder Abuse Helpline: 24/7; General: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Pennsylvania Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: FCC program providing low-income Pennsylvania residents a discount on monthly telephone or broadband internet service to stay connected.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals enrolled in qualifying assistance programs (Medicaid, SNAP, SSI, etc.) or meeting income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 135% of the Federal Poverty Level or participation in qualifying programs
